top_signals.title: BOJ’s Hawkish Board Member Calls for Rate Hike Every Few Months
top_signals.summary: The Bank of Japan needs to raise interest rates every few months and should quicken the pace in the face of stronger upside inflation risks, Naoki Tamura, one of the most hawkish board members, said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: US banks would lose $700bn in economic crash, Fed stress tests find
top_signals.summary: Several Wall Street lenders boost investor payouts after receiving pass grades
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: What’s at Stake If Indonesia Loses Its Emerging Market Status
top_signals.summary: Indonesia has spent decades establishing itself as one of the world’s most important emerging markets. Now the country — Southeast Asia’s largest economy, with a gross domestic product of about $1.5 trillion — risks losing that standing, potentially jeopardizing billions of dollars in foreign investment.

top_signals.title: Hong Kong booksellers arrested for alleged security law violations
Global Ebola risk ‘remains low,’ WHO chief says after France case
top_signals.summary: France announced its first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory: a doctor who had flown back from the Congo.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
